metadata.name: Frozen Manufacturing Knowledge Base for LCE-I5S Decision Support
metadata.purpose: Frozen, manually curated procedural knowledge base for a controlled LLM-based decision-support framework in manufacturing-system design. It supports generation for Product Transfer, Technology Transfer, and Facility Design scenarios, organised by LCE stage and Industry 5.0 I5S dimension.
important_boundary_conditions: This knowledge base is used for generation in the full-framework configuration only.
It is not a RAG index and does not perform runtime retrieval.
It excludes Ramírez, Cortés, and Molina's IPPMD-based lightweight manufacturing-system design framework because that framework is reserved as an external procedural benchmark.
It does not provide empirical validation, plant-floor performance evidence, or automatic implementation authority.

AI outputs must remain decision support; changes to SOPs, control plans, MES/ERP records, equipment parameters, supplier status, production settings, or operational records require human review and approval.

source_basis.source: Digital Technologies for Sustainable Manufacturing, Chapter 1
source_basis.use: LCE framing; lifecycle scope from materials/products/processes/systems/supply chains to end-of-life; integration of I5S principles.

source_basis.source: Digital Technologies for Sustainable Manufacturing, Chapter 2
source_basis.use: Definitions of Social, Sustainable, Sensing, Smart, and Safe systems and their lifecycle use from ideation to end-of-life.

source_basis.source: Digital Technologies for Sustainable Manufacturing, Chapter 6
source_basis.use: Manufacturing-system entity; Product Transfer, Technology Transfer, Facility Design; LCE stage activities, inputs, and outputs.

source_basis.source: Digital Technologies for Sustainable Manufacturing, Chapter 7
source_basis.use: Supply-chain development logic; resilience, supplier engagement, transparency, real-time decision support, and end-of-life recovery.

source_basis.source: Standard manufacturing engineering methods and safety/environmental management practice
source_basis.use: Representative tools and methods such as APQP, SPC, FMEA, HAZOP, LOTO, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, ISO 13849, IEC 62443, MES/MOM, SCADA, OPC-UA/MQTT, APS, digital twins, DES, CAD/BIM.

excluded_generation_sources: Ramírez, Cortés, and Molina IPPMD-based lightweight manufacturing-system design framework (reserved for external benchmark scoring, not generation).
Reviewer reports or benchmark scoring rubrics.
Unverified public web sources at runtime.
User-unprovided plant-floor numerical targets, deadlines, defect rates, recovery rates, or certified performance claims.
metadata.version_note: File names are stable. Versioning is stored in metadata and repository tags, not in the filename.

Social.definition: Human-centred and socially responsible manufacturing-system design, including ergonomics, training, inclusion, operator participation, fair work, and stakeholder engagement.
Social.evidence_rule: Increase expected maturity only when at least one concrete human-centred action or deliverable is present.

Sustainable.definition: Environmentally responsible manufacturing-system design across lifecycle stages, including resource efficiency, energy monitoring, LCA, circularity, reuse, recycling, and reverse logistics.
Sustainable.evidence_rule: Avoid claiming verified carbon or resource reductions unless measured/simulated evidence is provided.

Sensing.definition: Data capture and monitoring capability for manufacturing assets, processes, quality, safety, and lifecycle states.
Sensing.evidence_rule: Require concrete sensors, data pathways, monitoring targets, or data-collection deliverables.

Smart.definition: Decision-support, automation, analytics, simulation, optimization, and digital-twin capability that improves planning, control, diagnosis, and lifecycle learning without autonomous unapproved actuation.
Smart.evidence_rule: AI and digital twins may recommend or simulate; operational changes require human approval.

Safe.definition: Occupational, machine, process, environmental, and cyber-physical safety controls across the manufacturing-system lifecycle.
Safe.evidence_rule: Distinguish functional/machine safety from OT cybersecurity; do not use Safety as the I5S label, use Safe.

exclusion_rules: Do not claim industrial validation or plant-floor performance improvement from generated text alone.
Do not invent numerical targets, percentages, deadlines, defect rates, recovery rates, first-pass yield thresholds, carbon-reduction targets, or material-recovery percentages.
Do not use sector-specific standards such as ISO 13485 unless the selected industry or uploaded documentation explicitly justifies them.
Do not describe AI as automatically updating SOPs, control plans, MES/ERP records, equipment parameters, supplier status, production settings, maintenance actions, or operational records.
Do not use blockchain unless traceability specifically requires it; prefer auditable digital traceability or digital traceability system.
Use Industry 5.0 I5S labels Social, Sustainable, Sensing, Smart, and Safe; avoid using Safety as a dimension label.

Treat cyber-by-design, sensing, and digital traceability as support functions in Facility Design, not as replacements for layout, capacity, material flow, utilities, safety zones, commissioning, and reconfiguration logic.
Keep Ramírez et al. outside generation; use it only for external procedural benchmark scoring.

entries.core_activity: Define product information, BOM, materials, standards, quality requirements, and delivery expectations.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Capture market/customer requirements, preliminary design concepts, existing product data, and product-transfer constraints.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Product engineering, manufacturing engineering, quality, procurement, and supply-chain teams.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: BOM, material specification sheet, component standards, quality requirement matrix, estimated delivery needs, traceability needs.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: BOM tools, material databases, quality templates, stakeholder input, and supplier capability information.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: BOM and quality requirements approved as the basis for manufacturing and supplier planning.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Life Cycle diagram flow, Product Transfer description
Book ch.6, Idea | Imagination: Individual Component Specification
entries.prompt_priority: Define product information, BOM, materials, standards, quality requirements, and delivery expectations.

entries.core_activity: Define manufacturing and quality-control requirements for volume, cost, time, product attributes, tolerances, and acceptance variables.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Translate BOM and product attributes into manufacturing specifications and quality-control parameters.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Manufacturing engineering, process planning, quality assurance, supply-chain planning, and metrology support.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Manufacturing specification, quality-control parameters, inspection plan, acceptance criteria, control-variable definitions.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: APQP/SPC tools, process flow templates, metrology resources, inspection templates, and training materials.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Manufacturing and inspection plans reviewed for feasibility before supplier qualification and launch preparation.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Basic Development: Manufacturing and Quality Control Requirements
entries.prompt_priority: Define manufacturing and quality-control requirements for volume, cost, time, product attributes, tolerances, and acceptance variables.

entries.core_activity: Select manufacturing suppliers and partners based on capabilities, capacity, compliance, cost, quality, and risk.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Evaluate supplier capabilities and qualification evidence against manufacturing specifications.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Procurement, supply chain, quality, product engineering, and technical evaluation teams.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Supplier shortlist, qualification reports, audit evidence, supplier capability/capacity reports, risk assessment.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: AHP/TCO or similar MCDM tools, supplier data, audit checklists, capability matrix, and compliance screening.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Supplier selection supported by qualification evidence and documented sourcing decision.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Advanced Development: Manufacturing Supplier Selection
Book ch.7, supplier engagement and resilience
entries.prompt_priority: Select manufacturing suppliers and partners based on capabilities, capacity, compliance, cost, quality, and risk.

entries.core_activity: Manufacture, assemble, check quality controls, document delivery/inventory, train workforce, and manage ramp-up readiness.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Execute manufacturing and assembly instructions, quality checks, training, and launch-readiness verification.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Production team, quality manager, training coordinator, manufacturing engineering, and supply-chain team.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Assembly instructions, quality-control reports, acceptance criteria, training records, traceability log, delivery/inventory records.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Assembly equipment, training materials, inspection tools, APQP/SPC records, and digital traceability system.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Assembly readiness verified through acceptance criteria, training completion, quality-control evidence, traceability readiness, and ramp-up approval.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Launching: Manufacturing and Assembly of Components
entries.prompt_priority: Manufacture, assemble, check quality controls, document delivery/inventory, train workforce, and manage ramp-up readiness.

entries.core_activity: Plan disassembly, reuse, recycling, reverse logistics, contract closure, and product-transfer lifecycle documentation.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Assess end-of-life options for components/products and define recovery, reuse, and reverse-logistics pathways.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Sustainability, logistics, procurement, supplier management, product engineering, and quality teams.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Reverse-logistics plan, contract-closure checklist, disassembly instructions, recycling pathways, lessons learned.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Disassembly tools, recycling contracts, supplier records, lifecycle-data repository, and traceability system.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: End-of-life and contract-closure plan approved with reuse, recycling, and reverse-logistics pathways documented.
source_basis: Book ch.6, End of Life: 5S
Book ch.2, End-of-life 10R and 5S
entries.prompt_priority: Plan disassembly, reuse, recycling, reverse logistics, contract closure, and product-transfer lifecycle documentation.

entries.core_activity: Capture component or component-family specifications: geometry, drawings, materials, and production rates/batch size.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Capture technical specifications, geometry, material data, production-rate assumptions, and feasibility constraints.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Product engineering, manufacturing engineering, materials, quality, and technology specialists.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Technical specification sheet, geometric data/drawings, material data sheets, production batch-size documentation.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: CAD models, material databases, supplier capability data, and technical-economic benchmarking inputs.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Technical specification sheet approved by product and manufacturing engineering for downstream technology-selection decisions.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Idea | Imagination: Technology transfer Individual Component Specification
entries.prompt_priority: Capture component or component-family specifications: geometry, drawings, materials, and production rates/batch size.

entries.core_activity: Evaluate machines, material-handling equipment, tooling, jigs/fixtures, and supporting tools to select manufacturing resources.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Compare manufacturing technologies and equipment using technical-economic, feasibility, capacity, and sustainability criteria.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Manufacturing engineering, procurement, process engineering, quality, maintenance, and equipment suppliers.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Benchmark report, equipment/technology shortlist, supplier data, feasibility assumptions, pilot-line or joint-development trial plan.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Equipment catalogues, supplier trials, pilot-line resources, AHP/TCO or similar decision tools, feasibility-analysis templates.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Technology-selection decision supported by benchmark evidence, equipment shortlist, and human-approved feasibility review.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Basic Development: Manufacturing Resources Specification and Selection
Book ch.6, Ten Core Technologies
entries.prompt_priority: Evaluate machines, material-handling equipment, tooling, jigs/fixtures, and supporting tools to select manufacturing resources.

entries.core_activity: Develop process plan, SOPs, control documentation, inspection criteria, and data-collection logic for the selected technology.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Define process flow, raw materials, tools, fixtures, gauges, SOPs, quality-control process documentation, and MES/ERP data requirements.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Process engineering, quality assurance, automation/MES team, maintenance, and operations representatives.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Detailed process charts, control plan, SOP draft, inspection criteria, MES/ERP data requirements, pilot-validation evidence.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: SOP templates, quality tools, metrology resources, MES/ERP interface definitions, predictive-maintenance inputs, digital-twin/simulation support.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Process plan and control documentation validated for pilot testing with a human-approved SOP baseline.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Advanced Development: Process Plan
Book ch.6, Ten Core Technologies
entries.prompt_priority: Develop process plan, SOPs, control documentation, inspection criteria, and data-collection logic for the selected technology.

entries.core_activity: Set up, test, qualify, and ramp up equipment; conduct pilot production and first-article inspection.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Install, qualify, test, and ramp up equipment through installation qualification, pilot production, first-article inspection, and deviation review.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Commissioning team, operations, maintenance, quality, supplier technical support, and process engineering.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Installation qualification report, calibration records, ramp-up checklist, first-article inspection report, deviation log, approval evidence.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Installation tools, calibration equipment, pilot-line resources, inspection systems, MES/SCADA data, digital-twin/simulation support.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Equipment readiness confirmed through installation qualification, first-article inspection, and human-approved production ramp-up decision.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Launching: Equipment Set-up and Ramp-up of Production
entries.prompt_priority: Set up, test, qualify, and ramp up equipment; conduct pilot production and first-article inspection.

entries.core_activity: Retire or adapt equipment, document performance and failures, and update lifecycle-learning records for future transfers.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Assess equipment retirement/adaptation options, failure history, maintainability, reuse/recycling potential, and lessons learned.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Asset management, maintenance, sustainability/logistics, engineering knowledge management, and operations.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: End-of-life report, failure-history log, lessons-learned archive, digital-twin lifecycle update, reuse/recycling plan.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Maintenance records, MES/ERP or historian data, decommissioning tools, recycling/reuse partners, digital-twin records.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Closure approved after performance history, failure lessons, and reuse or retirement decisions are documented for future technology-transfer learning.
source_basis: Book ch.6, End of Life: 5S
Book ch.2, End-of-life 10R and 5S
entries.prompt_priority: Retire or adapt equipment, document performance and failures, and update lifecycle-learning records for future transfers.

entries.core_activity: Specify product, process, production-volume, and facility requirements to initiate new or transformed facility design.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Define product/process requirements, demand scenarios, facility constraints, site assumptions, and human-factors criteria.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Manufacturing engineering, operations, product engineering, facilities, safety, ergonomics, sustainability, and supply-chain stakeholders.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Facility requirements brief, process-flow assumptions, production-volume forecasts, site constraints, ergonomic criteria, sustainability criteria.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Process data, product documentation, site information, stakeholder workshops, preliminary capacity assumptions, ergonomic assessment data.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Facility requirements brief approved as the ideation gate for manufacturing-system and equipment selection.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Idea | Imagination: Facility Design Product and Manufacturing Process Specification
entries.prompt_priority: Specify product, process, production-volume, and facility requirements to initiate new or transformed facility design.

entries.core_activity: Select manufacturing systems, equipment, utilities, and facility concepts that satisfy process and capacity needs.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Evaluate available technology, machines, material handling equipment, tooling, jigs/fixtures, utility needs, and make-or-buy logic.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Facilities engineering, manufacturing engineering, procurement, operations, safety, sustainability, and finance/cost review.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Equipment/system specification sheet, utility matrix, system alternatives, make-or-buy logic, updated facility layout assumptions.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Supplier data, equipment catalogues, utility-load calculations, layout tools, capacity models, technical-economic benchmarking templates.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Facility concept and equipment/system selection reviewed against process, capacity, safety, sustainability, and utility requirements.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Basic Development: Manufacturing System Specification
entries.prompt_priority: Select manufacturing systems, equipment, utilities, and facility concepts that satisfy process and capacity needs.

entries.core_activity: Design shop-floor layout, capacity, material flow, manufacturing strategy, storage/buffers, human movement, safety zones, utilities, and digital infrastructure.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Develop integrated layout and manufacturing strategy using capacity plans, material-flow logic, station/cell configuration, safety zoning, and simulation evidence.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Industrial engineering, facilities, operations, maintenance, safety, IT/OT, logistics, quality, and sustainability teams.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Detailed layout drawings, capacity analysis, material-flow map, storage/buffer plan, safety-zone plan, utility plan, digital-infrastructure requirements.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Layout simulation, DES or capacity tools, CAD/BIM resources, safety assessment tools, logistics data, utility-planning inputs.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Layout and capacity design validated through flow, safety, utility, and operational-readiness review.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Advanced Development: Manufacturing System Design
Book ch.6, Ten Core Technologies
entries.prompt_priority: Design shop-floor layout, capacity, material flow, manufacturing strategy, storage/buffers, human movement, safety zones, utilities, and digital infrastructure.

entries.core_activity: Build, install, commission, calibrate, validate, ramp up, and evaluate the facility and manufacturing systems.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Execute construction/build, equipment installation, commissioning, calibration, site acceptance testing, operator readiness, safety sign-off, and ramp-up evaluation.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Construction/facilities team, equipment suppliers, commissioning team, operations, maintenance, quality, safety, IT/OT, and training coordinators.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Installation records, commissioning checklist, site acceptance test report, ramp-up evaluation checklist, training records, safety sign-off, first-article inspection records.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Construction resources, installation tools, commissioning protocols, calibration equipment, training materials, MES/SCADA infrastructure, inspection tools.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Facility readiness confirmed through commissioning, site acceptance, training, safety sign-off, and human-approved ramp-up decision.
source_basis: Book ch.6, Launching: Facility Construction, Set-up, and Ramp-up of Production
entries.prompt_priority: Build, install, commission, calibrate, validate, ramp up, and evaluate the facility and manufacturing systems.

entries.core_activity: Audit facility for reuse, reconfiguration, transformation, decommissioning, asset recovery, reverse logistics, and lessons learned.
entries.engineering_analysis_function: Assess reuse, transformation, decommissioning, asset recovery, reverse logistics, environmental impact, and facility adaptability options.
entries.engineering_synthesis_organization: Facilities, asset management, sustainability, finance, operations, safety, logistics, and decommissioning/reconfiguration partners.
entries.engineering_synthesis_information: Facility audit, asset inventory, reconfiguration options, decommissioning plan, sustainability assessment, reverse-logistics plan, lessons learned.
entries.engineering_synthesis_resource: Asset records, maintenance data, facility drawings, environmental records, audit checklists, decommissioning/reconfiguration partners.
entries.engineering_evaluation_performance: Reuse, reconfiguration, or decommissioning decision approved with documented asset, safety, and sustainability evidence.
source_basis: Book ch.6, End of Life: 5S
Book ch.2, End-of-life 10R and 5S
entries.prompt_priority: Audit facility for reuse, reconfiguration, transformation, decommissioning, asset recovery, reverse logistics, and lessons learned.
